items.title: Nearly half of kidney transplant patients never even get started
items.summary: A massive national study found that nearly half of Americans with kidney failure who are referred for a transplant never even begin the evaluation process, and only 19% make it onto the transplant waitlist. items.summary: A surprising discovery is overturning a long-held assumption about how the brain’s movement center works. items.title: Modern neuroscience is rediscovering an idea Freud had 130 years ago
items.summary: What if Sigmund Freud was onto something that modern neuroscience is only now beginning to explain? A new paper argues that today's leading theory of the brain—as a prediction machine constantly anticipating the world—closely mirrors ideas psychoanalysis has explored for more than a century.

items.title: Scientists discover a surprising link between vitamin C and brain health
items.summary: Could something as simple as vitamin C help support a healthier aging brain?
